message:
perf(pager): defer concurrent-mode page-one conflict tracking to commit surface

In concurrent transaction mode, page-one conflict tracking for allocate,
free, and high-page write operations was being pulled into the per-op
hot path, causing unnecessary contention on the MVCC conflict surface
during ordinary data mutations. Page 1 (db header / page count / freelist
pointer) only needs reconciliation at commit-publication time for
concurrent transactions.

Changes:
- allocate_page_requires_page_one_conflict_tracking: return false early
  for TransactionMode::Concurrent, since the leased allocator handles
  page growth locally and publishes page-count updates at commit time.

- free_page_requires_page_one_conflict_tracking: same early-out for
  concurrent mode; freelist reconciliation is a commit-planning concern.

- write_page_requires_page_one_conflict_tracking: when mode is Concurrent
  and page_no != ONE, return false. Direct writes to page 1 still track
  (explicit `if page_no == PageNumber::ONE { return true }` guard).

- Remove the `self.mode == TransactionMode::Concurrent` arm from the
  committed_freelist_is_snapshot_pinned check in allocate, since the
  early return now preempts it.

Update trait docstrings in traits.rs to mention commit-time-only metadata
updates alongside net-zero allocator churn.

Update two pager tests to assert the new deferred behavior: concurrent
durable-free and concurrent growth-write no longer require per-op
page-one conflict tracking, but pending_commit_pages() still contains
PageNumber::ONE (verifying the commit surface catches it).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
